description: Creates a closed polygon from three or more vertex points, automatically connecting consecutive vertices with edges and closing the shape.
utility: Create closed polygon from three or more points

arguments.name: points
arguments.type: point | point(G, x, y) | x, y pairs
arguments.description: Three or more points defining polygon vertices (can be point variables, inline points, or x,y coordinate pairs)

alternates.syntax: polygon(G, P1, P2, P3, P4, c(blue), fc(lightblue))
alternates.description: Colored polygon with stroke and fill
Regular polygon with given center point, circumradius, and number of sides (>= 3)

examples.scenario: Triangle from three inline points (two-tables lesson)
Feasible region polygon from origin and intersection points with fill opacity (linear programming lesson)

examples.expression: pentagon_1 = polygon(graph_1, P1, P2, P3, P4, P5, c(blue), fc(lightblue))
